records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1209 - Abu al-Maghra' Humayd ibn al-Muthanna said:
"I was with Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) when Hafs al-Kalbi asked him:
"When I am behind the Imam and he is reciting aloud, may I make supplications and seek refuge (in Allah (swt))?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, you may supplicate."
records.arabicText: 1209 - وَقَالَ أَبُو اَلْمَغْرَاءِ حُمَيْدُ بْنُ اَلْمُثَنَّى: كُنْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَسَأَلَهُ حَفْصٌ اَلْكَلْبِيُّ فَقَالَ أَكُونُ خَلْفَ اَلْإِمَامِ وَ هُوَ يَجْهَرُ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فَأَدْعُو وَ أَتَعَوَّذُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ فَادْعُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1209 - Abu al-Maghra' Humayd ibn al-Muthanna said:
"I was with Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) when Hafs al-Kalbi asked him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When I am behind the Imam and he is reciting aloud, may I make supplications and seek refuge (in Allah (swt))?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, you may supplicate."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1210 - Al-Hussain ibn Abdullah al-Arrajani narrated from Imam (as) who said:
"Whoever prays in his own mosque and then goes to one of their mosques and prays with them, he will leave with the reward of their good deeds."
records.arabicText: 1210 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ اَلْأَرَّجَانِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «مَنْ صَلَّى فِي مَسْجِدِهِ ثُمَّ أَتَى مَسْجِداً مِنْ مَسَاجِدِهِمْ فَصَلَّى مَعَهُمْ خَرَجَ بِحَسَنَاتِهِم.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1210 - Al-Hussain ibn Abdullah al-Arrajani narrated from Imam (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays in his own mosque and then goes to one of their mosques and prays with them, he will leave with the reward of their good deeds."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1211 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam (as) who said:
"There is no servant who prays at the prescribed time, completes it, then joins another group and prays with them while still in a state of wudu' except that Allah (swt) writes for him twenty-five degrees (levels of reward)."
records.arabicText: 1211 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يُصَلِّي فِي اَلْوَقْتِ وَ يَفْرُغُ ثُمَّ يَأْتِيهِمْ وَ يُصَلِّي مَعَهُمْ وَ هُوَ عَلَى وُضُوءٍ إِلاَّ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ خَمْساً وَ عِشْرِينَ دَرَجَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1211 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no servant who prays at the prescribed time, completes it, then joins another group and prays with them while still in a state of wudu' except that Allah (swt) writes for him twenty-five degrees (levels of reward)."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1212 - He also said to Imam (as):
"There is a mosque near my door in which there are people who oppose and are hostile. They remain in prayer during the evening, and I pray Asr and then go out and join them in prayer."
Imam (as) said: "Would you not be pleased to have it counted for you as twenty-four prayers?".
records.arabicText: 1212 - وَقَالَ لَهُ أَيْضاً: إِنَّ عَلَى بَابِي مَسْجِداً يَكُونُ فِيهِ قَوْمٌ مُخَالِفُونَ مُعَانِدُونَ فَهُمْ يُمْسُونَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ أَنَا أُصَلِّي اَلْعَصْرَ ثُمَّ أَخْرُجُ فَأُصَلِّي مَعَهُمْ فَقَالَ «أَ مَا تَرْضَى أَنْ تُحْسَبَ لَكَ، بِأَرْبَعٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ صَلاَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1212 - He also said to Imam (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is a mosque near my door in which there are people who oppose and are hostile. They remain in prayer during the evening, and I pray Asr and then go out and join them in prayer."
Imam (as) said: "Would you not be pleased to have it counted for you as twenty-four prayers?".

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1213 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If you pray with them, you will be forgiven to the extent of the number of those who oppose you."
records.arabicText: 1213 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ مَعَهُمْ غُفِرَ لَكَ بِعَدَدِ مَنْ خَالَفَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1213 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you pray with them, you will be forgiven to the extent of the number of those who oppose you."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1214 - Al-Halabi narrated from the Imam (as) from his father (as) who said:
"If you have already prayed and you are in the mosque, and the congregational prayer is established, then if you wish, you may leave, or if you wish, you may pray with them and consider it as a form of tasbih (remembrance and glorification)."
records.arabicText: 1214 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْهُ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ صَلاَةً وَ أَنْتَ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَأُقِيمَتِ اَلصَّلاَةُ فَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَاخْرُجْ وَ إِنْ شِئْتَ فَصَلِّ مَعَهُمْ وَ اِجْعَلْهَا تَسْبِيحاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1214 - Al-Halabi narrated from the Imam (as) from his father (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you have already prayed and you are in the mosque, and the congregational prayer is established, then if you wish, you may leave, or if you wish, you may pray with them and consider it as a form of tasbih (remembrance and glorification)."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1215 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"Pray and consider it for what you missed."
records.arabicText: 1215 - وَ رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «صَلِّ وَ اِجْعَلْهَا لِمَا فَاتَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1215 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Pray and consider it for what you missed."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1216 - Mu'awiyah ibn Sharih reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"If a person comes quickly while the Imam is in ruku' (bowing), one takbir (saying 'Allahu Akbar') suffices for him to enter the prayer and perform the ruku'."
records.arabicText: 1216 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ شُرَيْحٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا جَاءَ اَلرَّجُلُ مُبَادِراً وَ اَلْإِمَامُ رَاكِعٌ أَجْزَأَتْهُ تَكْبِيرَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ لِدُخُولِهِ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ اَلرُّكُوعِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1216 - Mu'awiyah ibn Sharih reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a person comes quickly while the Imam is in ruku' (bowing), one takbir (saying 'Allahu Akbar') suffices for him to enter the prayer and perform the ruku'."

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1217 Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abu Ali al-Harrani who said:
“We were with Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) when a man came and said:
"We prayed Fajr in the mosque, and some of us left while others stayed for tasbih (glorification). Then, a man entered the mosque and began to give the adhan. We prevented and stopped him from doing that."
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "You did well to stop and prevent him firmly."
I asked him, "What if a group enters?"
Imam (as) said: "They should pray in a corner of the mosque without appointing an Imam”.
records.arabicText: 1217 - فَقَدْ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَرَّانِيِّ قَالَ: كُنَّا عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ صَلَّيْنَا فِي مَسْجِدٍ اَلْفَجْرَ فَانْصَرَفَ بَعْضُنَا وَ جَلَسَ بَعْضٌ فِي اَلتَّسْبِيحِ فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْنَا رَجُلٌ اَلْمَسْجِدَ فَأَذَّنَ فَمَنَعْنَاهُ وَ دَفَعْنَاهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «أَحْسَنْتُمْ اِدْفَعُوهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ وَ اِمْنَعُوهُ أَشَدَّ اَلْمَنْعِ» فَقُلْتُ لَهُ فَإِنْ دَخَلَ جَمَاعَةٌ فَقَالَ «يَقُومُونَ فِي نَاحِيَةِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ وَ لاَ يَبْدُو لَهُمْ إِمَامٌ.

records.chapter: Congregational Prayer and Its Virtue
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1218 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Jamil ibn Salih, from Sama’a, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who was behind the Imam and missed one rak'ah, but then the Imam mistakenly performed five rak'ahs.
Imam (as) said: "He (the follower) should make up the missed rak'ah and should not count the Imam's mistake”.
records.arabicText: 1218 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي رَجُلٍ سَبَقَهُ اَلْإِمَامُ بِرَكْعَةٍ ثُمَّ أَوْهَمَ اَلْإِمَامُ فَصَلَّى خَمْساً قَالَ «يَقْضِي تِلْكَ اَلرَّكْعَةَ وَ لاَ يَعْتَدُّ بِوَهَمِ اَلْإِمَامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1218 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Jamil ibn Salih, from Sama’a, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who was behind the Imam and missed one rak'ah, but then the Imam mistakenly performed five rak'ahs.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He (the follower) should make up the missed rak'ah and should not count the Imam's mistake”.

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"There are four prayers that a person may perform at any hour: a prayer that you missed, so whenever you remember it, you perform it; the two units (rak'ahs) of the obligatory circumambulation (Tawaf) prayer; the prayer for an eclipse (Salat al-Kusuf); and the prayer over a deceased person (Salat al-Janazah). These prayers can be performed by a person at any time."
records.arabicText: رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «أَرْبَعُ صَلَوَاتٍُ يُصَلِّيهَا اَلرَّجُلُ فِي كُلِّ سَاعَةٍ صَلاَةٌ فَاتَتْكَ فَمَتَى مَا ذَكَرْتَهَا أَدَّيْتَهَا وَ صَلاَةُ رَكْعَتَيْ طَوَافِ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ وَ صَلاَةُ اَلْكُسُوفِ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ هَذِهِ يُصَلِّيهِنَّ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي اَلسَّاعَاتِ كُلِّهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Zurarah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There are four prayers that a person may perform at any hour: a prayer that you missed, so whenever you remember it, you perform it; the two units (rak'ahs) of the obligatory circumambulation (Tawaf) prayer; the prayer for an eclipse (Salat al-Kusuf); and the prayer over a deceased person (Salat al-Janazah). These prayers can be performed by a person at any time."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1219 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said to Zurarah ibn A'yan:
"Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has enjoined upon people thirty-five prayers from one Jumu’ah to the next, one of which Allah (swt) has made obligatory in congregation — that is, the Jumu’ah prayer.
It is waived for nine categories: the young, the elderly, the insane, the traveler, the slave, the woman, the sick, the blind, and anyone who is more than two farsakhs (about six miles) away.
Recitation in it must be done aloud, and ghusl (ritual washing) is obligatory for it. The Imam is required to perform two Qunoot (special supplications): one in the first rak'ah before bowing (ruku') and one in the second rak'ah after bowing."
records.arabicText: 1219 - قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِزُرَارَةَ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ: «إِنَّمَا فَرَضَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى اَلنَّاسِ مِنَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ إِلَى اَلْجُمُعَةِ خَمْساً وَ ثَلاَثِينَ صَلاَةً مِنْهَا صَلاَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَرَضَهَا اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ وَ هِيَ اَلْجُمُعَةُ وَ وَضَعَهَا عَنْ تِسْعَةٍ عَنِ اَلصَّغِيرِ وَ اَلْكَبِيرِ وَ اَلْمَجْنُونِ وَ اَلْمُسَافِرِ وَ اَلْعَبْدِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ وَ اَلْمَرِيضِ وَ اَلْأَعْمَى وَ مَنْ كَانَ عَلَى رَأْسِ فَرْسَخَيْنِ وَ اَلْقِرَاءَةُ فِيهَا بِالْجَهْرِ وَ اَلْغُسْلُ فِيهَا وَاجِبٌ وَ عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ فِيهَا قُنُوتَانِ قُنُوتٌ فِي اَلرَّكْعَةِ اَلْأُولَى قَبْلَ اَلرُّكُوعِ وَ فِي اَلرَّكْعَةِ اَلثَّانِيَةِ بَعْدَ اَلرُّكُوعِ.»

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1221 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The two rak'ahs that The Prophet (sw) added on the day of Jumu’ah are for those who attend with the Imam because of the two sermons. Whoever prays on Jumu’ah but not in congregation should pray it as four rak'ahs, like the Dhuhr prayer on other days."
records.arabicText: 1221 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّمَا وُضِعَتِ اَلرَّكْعَتَانِ اَللَّتَانِ أَضَافَهُمَا اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ لِلْمُقِيمِ لِمَكَانِ اَلْخُطْبَتَيْنِ مَعَ اَلْإِمَامِ فَمَنْ صَلَّى يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فِي غَيْرِ جَمَاعَةٍ فَلْيُصَلِّهَا أَرْبَعاً كَصَلاَةِ اَلظُّهْرِ فِي سَائِرِ اَلْأَيَّامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1221 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The two rak'ahs that The Prophet (sw) added on the day of Jumu’ah are for those who attend with the Imam because of the two sermons. Whoever prays on Jumu’ah but not in congregation should pray it as four rak'ahs, like the Dhuhr prayer on other days."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1222 - The Imam (as) said:
"The time for the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is on Jumu’ah at the hour when the sun begins to decline (zawal). Its timing is the same for both travelers and residents and is considered limited (specific). The Asr prayer on Jumu’ah is performed at the time of Dhuhr on other days."
records.arabicText: 1222 - وقال عليه السلام: " وقت صلاة الجمعة يوم الجمعة ساعة تزول الشمس ، ووقتها في السفر والحضر واحد وهو من المضيق، وصلاة العصر يوم الجمعة في الأولى في سائر الأيام.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1222 - The Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The time for the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is on Jumu’ah at the hour when the sun begins to decline (zawal). Its timing is the same for both travelers and residents and is considered limited (specific). The Asr prayer on Jumu’ah is performed at the time of Dhuhr on other days."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1223 - bd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"There is no harm in leaving the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) due to rain."
records.arabicText: 1223 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَدَعَ اَلْجُمُعَةَ فِي اَلْمَطَرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1223 - bd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in leaving the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) due to rain."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1224 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"The Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is obligatory upon seven believers: the Imam, his judge, one who claims a right, two witnesses, and the one who executes punishments in front of the Imam. It is not obligatory for fewer than these."
records.arabicText: 1224 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «تَجِبُ اَلْجُمُعَةُ عَلَى سَبْعَةِ نَفَرٍ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ لاَ تَجِبُ عَلَى أَقَلَّ مِنْهُمُ اَلْإِمَامُ وَ قَاضِيهِ وَ مُدَّعِيَا حَقٍّ وَ شَاهِدَانِ وَ اَلَّذِي يَضْرِبُ اَلْحُدُودَ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اَلْإِمَامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1224 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is obligatory upon seven believers: the Imam, his judge, one who claims a right, two witnesses, and the one who executes punishments in front of the Imam. It is not obligatory for fewer than these."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1225 - Imam Abu Ja'far (as) said:
"The beginning time for the Jumu’ah prayer is when the sun declines (zawal) until an hour has passed. Maintain it carefully, for the Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
'No servant asks Allah (swt) for good at this time except that He grants it.'"
records.arabicText: 1225 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ وَقْتِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ سَاعَةَ تَزُولُ اَلشَّمْسُ إِلَى أَنْ تَمْضِيَ سَاعَةٌ فَحَافِظْ عَلَيْهَا فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ «لاَ يَسْأَلُ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَبْدٌ فِيهَا خَيْراً إِلاَّ أَعْطَاهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1225 - Imam Abu Ja'far (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The beginning time for the Jumu’ah prayer is when the sun declines (zawal) until an hour has passed. Maintain it carefully, for the Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
'No servant asks Allah (swt) for good at this time except that He grants it.'"

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1226 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ali ibn Yaqtin who said:
"I asked Imam Abu al-Hasan (as) about the Jumu’ah prayer while traveling. What should I recite in them?"
Imam (as) said: "Recite in them 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas)."
records.arabicText: 1226 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَقْطِينٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ مَا أَقْرَأُ فِيهِمَا قَالَ «اِقْرَأْ فِيهِمَا «قُلْ هُوَ اَللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1226 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ali ibn Yaqtin who said:
"I asked Imam Abu al-Hasan (as) about the Jumu’ah prayer while traveling. What should I recite in them?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Recite in them 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas)."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1227 - Ja'far ibn Bashir and Abdullah ibn Jabalah narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Imam Abu Abdullah (as): "I heard him say: 'In the Jumu’ah prayer, there is no problem in reciting other than Surah al-Jumu'ah and Surah al-Munafiqun if you are in a hurry.'"
records.arabicText: 1227 - وَ رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ وَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ جَبَلَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: «فِي صَلاَةِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَقْرَأَ فِيهَا بِغَيْرِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ اَلْمُنَافِقِينَ إِذَا كُنْتَ مُسْتَعْجِلاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1227 - Ja'far ibn Bashir and Abdullah ibn Jabalah narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Imam Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I heard him say: 'In the Jumu’ah prayer, there is no problem in reciting other than Surah al-Jumu'ah and Surah al-Munafiqun if you are in a hurry.'"

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1228 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) used to prepare on Thursday for the Jumu’ah prayer.
records.arabicText: 1228 - وَ كَانَ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ يَتَهَيَّأُ يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ لِلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1228 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) used to prepare on Thursday for the Jumu’ah prayer.

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1229 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"The time for the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is at the sun's decline (zawal), and the time for the Dhuhr prayer while traveling is also at the sun's decline. The time for the Asr prayer on Jumu’ah for those who are not traveling is approximately the same as the Dhuhr prayer on other days."
records.arabicText: 1229 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «وَقْتُ اَلْجُمُعَةِ زَوَالُ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ وَقْتُ صَلاَةِ اَلظُّهْرِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ زَوَالُ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ وَقْتُ اَلْعَصْرِ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فِي اَلْحَضَرِ نَحْوٌ مِنْ وَقْتِ اَلظُّهْرِ فِي غَيْرِ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1229 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The time for the Jumu’ah prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) is at the sun's decline (zawal), and the time for the Dhuhr prayer while traveling is also at the sun's decline. The time for the Asr prayer on Jumu’ah for those who are not traveling is approximately the same as the Dhuhr prayer on other days."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1230 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "There is to be no speaking while the Imam is delivering the sermon, and no turning around except as is permissible during prayer. The Jumu’ah prayer was made two rak'ahs because of the two sermons, which have taken the place of the last two rak'ahs. Therefore, it remains as part of the prayer until the Imam descends (finishes the sermon)."
records.arabicText: 1230 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ كَلاَمَ وَ اَلْإِمَامُ يَخْطُبُ وَ لاَ اِلْتِفَاتَ إِلاَّ كَمَا يَحِلُّ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ إِنَّمَا جُعِلَتِ اَلْجُمُعَةُ رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِنْ أَجْلِ اَلْخُطْبَتَيْنِ جُعِلَتَا مَكَانَ اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ اَلْأَخِيرَتَيْنِ فَهِيَ صَلاَةٌ حَتَّى يَنْزِلَ اَلْإِمَامُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1230 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is to be no speaking while the Imam is delivering the sermon, and no turning around except as is permissible during prayer. The Jumu’ah prayer was made two rak'ahs because of the two sermons, which have taken the place of the last two rak'ahs. Therefore, it remains as part of the prayer until the Imam descends (finishes the sermon)."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1231 - Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There is no harm if a person speaks after the Imam has finished the sermon on Jumu’ah, during the time between the sermon and the establishment of the prayer, whether he hears the recitation (during the prayer) or not; it is sufficient for him."
records.arabicText: 1231 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَتَكَلَّمَ اَلرَّجُلُ إِذَا فَرَغَ اَلْإِمَامُ مِنَ اَلْخُطْبَةِ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ، مَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ أَنْ تُقَامَ اَلصَّلاَةُ وَ إِنْ سَمِعَ اَلْقِرَاءَةَ أَوْ لَمْ يَسْمَعْ أَجْزَأَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1231 - Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a person speaks after the Imam has finished the sermon on Jumu’ah, during the time between the sermon and the establishment of the prayer, whether he hears the recitation (during the prayer) or not; it is sufficient for him."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1232 - Sama’a narrated from Imam (as) who said:
"The Jumu’ah prayer with the Imam consists of two rak'ahs, but whoever prays it alone, it is four rak'ahs."
records.arabicText: 1232 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «صَلاَةُ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ مَعَ اَلْإِمَامِ رَكْعَتَانِ فَمَنْ صَلَّى وَحْدَهُ فَهِيَ أَرْبَعُ رَكَعَاتٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1232 - Sama’a narrated from Imam (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Jumu’ah prayer with the Imam consists of two rak'ahs, but whoever prays it alone, it is four rak'ahs."

records.chapter: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1233 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Imran Al-Halabi, who said:
"Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who prays the Jumu’ah prayer as four rak'ahs. Should he recite aloud?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, and there is Qunoot (a supplication) in the second rak'ah."
records.arabicText: 1233 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عِمْرَانَ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي اَلْجُمُعَةَ أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ أَ يَجْهَرُ فِيهَا بِالْقِرَاءَةِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ اَلْقُنُوتُ فِي اَلثَّانِيَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1233 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Imran Al-Halabi, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who prays the Jumu’ah prayer as four rak'ahs. Should he recite aloud?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, and there is Qunoot (a supplication) in the second rak'ah."
